# Copilot Instructions for DibyajyotiBiswal57/programs

## Repository Purpose

A polyglot collection of 89+ programming challenges (from `questions.md`) solved in 17+ languages. Each problem has a canonical filename used across all languages.

## Repository Structure

```
programs/
├── questions.md          # Authoritative list of all problems with filenames
├── status.md             # Completion status per problem/language
├── generate_html.py      # Generates index.html from questions.md + status.md
├── generate_status_json.py
├── index.html            # Auto-generated — do NOT edit manually
├── <language>/           # One folder per language (see below)
│   └── 0000_filename.ext
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        ├── lint-and-fix.yml    # Auto-formats code on push/PR
        ├── update-html.yml     # Runs generate_html.py, commits index.html
        └── update-status.yml
```

**Language folders:** `asm`, `batch`, `c`, `cpp`, `csharp`, `elixir`, `fortran`, `go`, `haskell`, `java`, `lua`, `perl`, `pwsh`, `python`, `qbasic`, `ruby`, `vb`

## File Naming Convention

All solution files follow the convention defined in `questions.md`:

```
0001_hello_world.py     # Python
0001_hello_world.java   # Java
0001_hello_world.c      # C
```

The four-digit prefix matches the problem number. Filenames (without extension) must match exactly what is listed in `questions.md`. **Never invent filenames** — always look up the correct name in `questions.md`.

## Adding Solutions

1. Find the problem's filename in `questions.md` (e.g., `[Filename - 0042_fibonacci]`).
2. Create the file in the appropriate language folder with the correct extension.
3. Include a brief comment at the top of the file describing what the program does.
4. Use 4-space indentation for Java, Python, and C#.
5. For Java, use `class` (not `public class`) so the class name need not match the filename.
6. Do **not** edit `index.html` directly — it is auto-generated by `generate_html.py`.

## CI/CD Workflows

- **`lint-and-fix.yml`**: Runs on every push/PR. Auto-formats code using `black`/`autopep8`/`isort` (Python), `prettier` (JS/HTML/CSS/SCSS), `google-java-format` (Java), `clang-format` (C/C++), `gofmt` (Go), `rubocop` (Ruby), `ormolu` (Haskell), and others. Commits fixes automatically on non-fork PRs.
- **`update-html.yml`**: Runs `python3 generate_html.py` and commits `index.html` if changed.
- **`update-status.yml`**: Updates `status.md` based on current files.

All formatters run with `continue-on-error: true`, so formatting failures do not block the workflow.

## Key Files

- **`questions.md`**: Master list of all problems. Each entry includes the problem statement and the canonical filename (without extension).
- **`status.md`**: Tracks which problems are solved (✅), in beta (❗️), or unfinished (❌) for each language.
- **`generate_html.py`**: Reads `questions.md` and `status.md` to generate the interactive `index.html` status page. Run with `python3 generate_html.py` to regenerate locally.
- **`CONTRIBUTING.md`**: Full contribution guidelines including the status legend and PR process.

## Critical Files — Do Not Modify

Do not modify `index.html`, `generate_html.py`, `generate_status_json.py`, `update_status.py`, workflow files, or any file in `.github/` unless the task explicitly requires it.
